danger_signals.visual_form: Anthropomorphic dog characters with criminal gang aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Criminal antagonists controlled by boss Mugsy; immediate combat threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Crime boss dog (visual design implied as distinct/threatening)
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist; represents kidnapping threat and criminal control of New York
Physical danger from stage design; progression barriers

safety_signals.visual_form: TV-style menu interface for purchasing power-ups
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone/break point; resource acquisition opportunity; temporary reprieve from combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Separated game mode with roulette, pipe toss, basketball challenges
safety_signals.meaning: Safe space for optional resource gathering without enemy threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Anthropomorphic 'rockin' cat' with punch gun weapon
safety_signals.meaning: Player-controlled entity; established as hero rescuing girlfriend from criminal threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Jazz musician cat; punch gun weapon
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: New York City setting; nightlife/performance cultural context
Carnival, airplane, park—locations occupied/controlled by gang; implied urban crime takeover of NYC
Motivation for progression; rescued at narrative end

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Criminal occupation of New York City
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seven themed 'channels' (TV metaphor) represent different districts/locations under gang control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies occupy each stage; Mugsy controls progression as final boss; player must traverse occupied territories

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Channel selection system; completing four optional channels unlocks fifth mandatory channel; secret stage unlocks post-credits
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty escalates; secret stage strips power-ups and increases challenge to test mastery; health no longer replenishes between levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three lives only; no money; no power-ups available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vastly increased difficulty; complete reliance on punch gun mechanics; no safety margin for error

environmental_storytelling.example: Game presented as 'channels' on a television set; reinforces idea of observing criminal underworld as spectacle; suggests media/entertainment framing of crime narrative
Carnival, airplane, park locations signal different gang-controlled territories; variety suggests gang penetration across urban infrastructure
Commercial break-style channel where player acquires power-ups; mirrors real TV shopping; suggests economy/consumerism as survival mechanic
Roulette, pipe toss, basketball presented as entertainment; mirrors game show aesthetic; reinforces TV/media framing

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Kidnaps Willy's girlfriend; controls criminal gang; occupies final channel
Occupy stages; attacked by player's punch gun; indicate gang presence/control
Hangs upside down and throws mini-bats; original design intended flight capability (visual tell of rejected mechanic)
Launches punch gun; hooks and swings from punch gun; grabs and throws objects; recovers health between non-final stages
Health not replenished after levels; no power-up access; three lives total; demonstrates mastery requirement

notes: Rockin' Kats uses a TV-channel framing device as its primary world-state communication method. The 'criminal occupation' narrative is expressed through themed stages representing different gang-controlled territories rather than explicit visual corruption indicators. Faction distinction is based on species (cat protagonist vs. dog antagonists), reflecting classic cartoon tropes. Safety is signaled through menu-based channels (shopping, bonus games) separate from action stages. The secret final stage's difficulty spike and resource removal communicate a 'everything you learned is stripped away' world-state shift. Limited sources available; actual sprite/color data not documented in provided sources.
